truthAudit.Summary: The previous analysis (Run 2) successfully identified 6 out of the 20 provided true vulnerabilities. It correctly located the memory safety issues (SC-V3-005, SC-V3-009), a race condition (SC-V3-006), a logic bypass (SC-V3-007), path traversal (SC-V3-008), and format string injection (SC-V3-001). However, it missed all other critical and high-severity flaws, including several UAF and integer overflow conditions. Notably, it reported a command injection vulnerability at a different site than the one specifically listed in the ground truth (SC-V3-004), which counts as a miss for the specific benchmark ID.

Items.Notes: The run correctly identified the use of a runtime-configurable string as the format argument for printf.
The analysis did not identify the integer overflow in the factorial computation result *= i.
The analysis missed the Use-After-Free involving the deferred history flush and memory pool cleanup.
Although the run reported a command injection via 'exec', it missed the specific command injection site in 'validate_config' anchored by 'validator_cmd + " " + config_file'.
Exactly identified the stack buffer overflow triggered when the expresson length exceeds or equals the buffer size.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: long long old_value = value_; std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(mutex_); value_ = old_value + 1;
Items.Notes: Correctly identified the race condition where value_ is read outside the lock scope.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: if (input.find("EMERGENCY_OVERRIDE") != std::string::npos) { authenticated_ = true; session_token_ = "EMERGENCY_SESSION"; ... return true; }
Items.Notes: Correctly identified the authentication bypass logic bomb.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: std::string full_path = config_dir_ + filename; if (!std::filesystem::exists(full_path)) return false; std::ifstream file(full_path);
Items.Notes: Correctly identified the path traversal in config loading.
Correctly identified the heap overflow in the expression buffer within the parser.
The hardcoded ADMIN_SECRET was not reported.
The use of std::mt19937 for session tokens was not reported as a weakness.
The thread-unsafe static tm cache in the logger was not identified.
Insecure temporary file handling via system() in append_override_audit_event was not identified.
Persistent authentication state in the admin session was not reported.
The missing exception handling around std::stod in handle_variable_command was not identified.
The integer underflow in the memory pool block split was not identified.
Unbounded recursion in the recursive descent parser was not reported.
Data race in evaluate_cached result_cache_ access was not reported.
The TOCTOU vulnerability in config loading was not reported.
Division by zero in calculate_route_score was not reported.
